subject: Clean-up persisted config
commit_message:
Clean-up persisted config

Those configuration files are persisted, hence ended up in the repo,
but in reality, those will be created by the application on startup.

- Remove MR logs (both DCAE and standalone ones)
- Remove generated MySQL files from Policy

Change-Id: I6b89d1a9e278456ca822d1b0d9c59ea9bc4df785
Issue-Id: OOM-322
Signed-off-by: Alexis de Talhouët <adetalhouet89@gmail.com>

entries.message:
Patch Set 1:

Hello Pamela, in this patch I'm removing a lot of generated MySQL files for the policy application. It seems a lot of those files are MySQL backup files, .frm, .myi and .myd.

Can you confirm those aren't required at the start of the application?

Patch Set 1: Verified-1

The message-router docker-compose checks these files out from git and mounts them into the docker container.  I believe these seed the dmaap topics required by the demo.
What we could do instead is an init container that checks out these files as we do not modify these at all - afaik they just need to be there.

The policy db has a pre-installed db in the image.  we can remove these but we will have to remove our peristent volume and risk policy database loss in the event of a failure.  If you remove these datafiles from the mount, then the oom policy db will not start.
See: https://gerrit.onap.org/r/#/c/10565/
I am not sure how we proceed here.
